text.title: Vertex AI Enterprise Integration
text.summary: Vertex AI provides enterprise features (CMEK, VPC, IAM, audit logs, model tuning) for Gemini; uses Application Default Credentials, not GOOGLE_API_KEY.

rule.statement: Vertex AI uses Application Default Credentials (gcloud auth application-default login), not GOOGLE_API_KEY — these are not interchangeable.
rule.rationale: Mixing auth methods causes silent 403 errors; Vertex AI IAM roles are applied to the service account, not to an API key.

example.code:
import vertexai from vertexai.generative_models import GenerativeModel, Part
vertexai.init(project="your-gcp-project", location="us-central1") model = GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro") response = model.generate_content("Explain quantum computing")

from vertexai.generative_models import Part
video_part = Part.from_uri(uri="gs://your-bucket/video.mp4", mime_type="video/mp4") model = GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro") response = model.generate_content(["Summarize this video", video_part])

list.item: VPC Service Controls: network isolation — prevent data exfiltration
CMEK: Customer-Managed Encryption Keys for data at rest
Cloud Audit Logs: every API call logged to Cloud Logging
IAM: fine-grained access control per model and project
Model Tuning: supervised fine-tuning on Vertex AI datasets
Evaluation: built-in evaluation metrics for model quality

rule.statement: Use Vertex AI when the deployment requires CMEK, VPC isolation, or SOC2/HIPAA compliance — the Gemini Developer API does not provide these guarantees.
rule.rationale: Enterprise compliance requirements are contractual; the Developer API is designed for prototyping, not regulated data handling.
